summary: Recall command human output (class legend + disclaimer guardrail) must render even when stdout is piped; machine JSON is emitted only on an explicit --json/--agent request, never on a bare non-terminal stdout.
reason: The generated recall search branch auto-switched to the machine JSON envelope whenever stdout was not a TTY (`!isTerminal(...) && !csv && !quiet && !plain`). Piping plain-text output (e.g. `check aspirin | head`) therefore bypassed the human-format branch that prints the FDA class legend and the 'not medical advice' disclaimer — the health-data safety contract. The check gates on explicit machine-format intent (--json/--agent) instead of TTY detection, so piped plain-text consumers keep the guardrail. Same fix applied to the no-records path for consistency.
validated_outcome: `check aspirin | cat` shows the class legend + disclaimer; `check aspirin --json | cat` still emits the raw openFDA envelope; no-records piped output stays text + disclaimer. go test ./internal/cli passes.
